description: README §5 STRUCTURE lists only 10 references and §6 SCRIPTS lists only 8 scripts, but the skill ships 15 references and 14 scripts. Undocumented references: candidate_proposal_format, mixed_executor_methodology, profiling_audit_log, promotion_gate_contract, score_dimensions. Undocumented scripts: benchmark-stability, candidate-lineage, improvement-journal, materialize-benchmark-fixtures, mutation-coverage, trade-off-detector (all present in SKILL.md §8 but absent from README tables).
recommended_fix: Phase-3 README rewrite regenerates the structure section from the live directory so all 15 references and 14 scripts appear. RESOLVED: §4 STRUCTURE now lists 15 references + 14 scripts.

description: README §3 FEATURES subsection numbering jumps from 3.6 (DIMENSIONAL PROGRESS TRACKING) to 3.8 (LEGAL-STOP EVENTS) with no 3.7.
recommended_fix: Phase-3 README rewrite renumbers FEATURES subsections contiguously. RESOLVED: new §3 uses contiguous 3.1-3.5.

description: SKILL.md is 544 LOC, exceeding the 500-LOC skill cap (the 600-LOC allowance is a system-spec-kit-only exception per feedback_skill_md_size_caps). Over-length is partly driven by the Mixed-Executor + Adjudication-Iter sections (~lines 255-316) that duplicate content already in references/mixed_executor_methodology.md.
recommended_fix: Dedicated trim packet: condense the duplicated Mixed-Executor/Adjudication prose to a pointer to references/mixed_executor_methodology.md (progressive-disclosure), bringing SKILL.md under 500 LOC.

description: SKILL.md section numbering is irregular: between '## 4. SUCCESS CRITERIA' and '## 5. RULES' sit five non-standard H2s — '## Mixed-Executor Dispatch (Recommended)', '## Adjudication-Iter Pattern (Recommended)', '## 4B. RUNTIME TRUTH CONTRACTS', '## Journal Wiring Contract', '## ADR-002: Journal Replay Consumer'. skill_md_template prescribes contiguous numbered ALL-CAPS H2 sections.
recommended_fix: Renumber/restructure: fold the two '(Recommended)' sections into §3 HOW IT WORKS as H3 subsections (content already lives in references/mixed_executor_methodology.md) and renumber the runtime-truth/journal sections contiguously.

description: SKILL.md (a runtime-state doc) cited mutable spec/phase/packet identifiers, violating the sk-doc evergreen packet-ID rule: 'arc 119' (x4: lines ~257/278/288/310), a volatile spec path '.opencode/specs/.../131-deep-skill-evolution/004-deep-research/001-uplift.../research-report.md' (line ~278), '(Phase 005)' in the 4B heading (line ~318), and '(Packet 110, M-3)' (line ~383). All occurrences are in §3+ (the Smart Router §2 was NOT touched, so ADR-004 holds and no ADR-005 is required).
evidence: post-fix re-grep shows zero of these mutable IDs remain in SKILL.md
recommended_fix: Replace each mutable ID with present-tense phrasing and a stable source anchor (references/mixed_executor_methodology.md). RESOLVED via 8 surgical edits.

description: Section heading used title-case '## 1. Overview' instead of the ALL-CAPS '## 1. OVERVIEW' used by the skill_reference_template and the other 13 references in this skill.
recommended_fix: Rename heading to ALL-CAPS. RESOLVED via Edit.

description: Section heading used title-case '## 1. Overview' instead of the ALL-CAPS '## 1. OVERVIEW' convention.
recommended_fix: Rename heading to ALL-CAPS. RESOLVED via Edit.

description: Evergreen packet-ID citations persist in reference + playbook docs: references/mixed_executor_methodology.md (arc 119 + volatile 131-arc spec paths in Precedent/Source/Related sections), references/profiling_audit_log.md (Packet 128/123, DAI-004), references/candidate_proposal_format.md (packet/spec citations), and manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-truth/034-replay-consumer.md. Changelog citations are NOT flagged (changelogs are the legitimate home for packet/phase references).
recommended_fix: Reword arc/packet citations to present-tense and replace volatile spec paths with stable anchors. RESOLVED (operator override at the phase-4 gate): reworded across references/mixed_executor_methodology.md, references/profiling_audit_log.md, references/candidate_proposal_format.md, manual_testing_playbook/07--runtime-truth/034-replay-consumer.md, and scripts/lib/README.md (a case-insensitive re-sweep caught the lowercase 'packet 124' the original Packet-cased grep missed). A cross-skill sweep of sibling deep-* skills remains recommended as a separate follow-on, out of this packet's scope.

description: CORRECTED FROM FALSE POSITIVE. Per-file line-1 inspection of all 10 changelogs shows 9 are correctly summary-first (no YAML frontmatter) and only v1.5.0.0.md carries frontmatter, deviating from the skill's dominant and sk-doc-correct summary-first convention. The initial 'grep -l ^--- = 9 files' reading was a false positive: it matched section-divider lines, not line-1 frontmatter. v1.4.0.0.md opens with a '# v1.4.0.0' version H1, a minor stylistic point left as-is.
evidence: head -1 of each changelog/v*.md: only v1.5.0.0.md equals '---'
v1.6.0.0.md (current head) starts with a summary paragraph
recommended_fix: Strip the YAML frontmatter from v1.5.0.0.md so it starts at its existing version H1, matching the other 9 changelogs. RESOLVED via Edit.
